what: Two ways to inject hook values
title: Advanced hooks: useBlocker and useOutletContext
text: Every hook wrapper reactRouter offers two mutually exclusive ways turn hook value UI. Pick whichever ergonomic case hand — API identical across hooks (useLoaderData(), useParams(), useBlocker(), useOutletContext(), …). 1. R-idiomatic (+ + selector) — default. hook value cloned existing component single prop. Good displaying one field, populating data grid’s rows, feeding input’s value: 2. Native render-prop (render = JS(...)) — escape hatch. Pass JS function (value) => ReactNode. Mirrors official React Router pattern right choice one prop enough — e.g. combining several fields, conditional rendering, template strings: render supplied, , , selector ignored — apply selection inside JS function directly.

code: useBlocker(   tags$span(),   shouldBlock = JS(     "({ currentLocation, nextLocation }) =>       currentLocation.pathname !== nextLocation.pathname"   ) ) # renders "unblocked", "blocked", or "proceeding"

title: Advanced hooks: useBlocker and useOutletContext
text: selector = "state" exposes state string, use render need branch full blocker object (e.g. show target URL confirmation prompt):
code: useBlocker(   shouldBlock = should_block,   render = JS(     "b => b.state === 'blocked'       ? `Leave for ${b.location.pathname}?`       : null"   ) )

title: Advanced hooks: useBlocker and useOutletContext
text: render function receives full blocker object, including b.proceed() b.reset() methods. Use inside onClick handlers build real confirmation dialog:
code: library(reactRouter) library(htmltools)  should_block <- JS(   "({ currentLocation, nextLocation }) =>     currentLocation.pathname !== nextLocation.pathname" )  blocker_ui <- JS(   "b => {     const state = React.createElement('code', null, b.state);     if (b.state !== 'blocked') return state;     return React.createElement(React.Fragment, null,       state, ' — ',       React.createElement('button', { onClick: () => b.proceed() }, 'Leave'),       ' ',       React.createElement('button', { onClick: () => b.reset() }, 'Stay')     );   }" )  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       element = div(         tags$nav(tags$ul(           tags$li(NavLink(to = "/",      "Home")),           tags$li(NavLink(to = "/other", "Other page"))         )),         tags$hr(),         Outlet()       ),       Route(         index = TRUE,         element = div(           tags$h3("Home — blocker active"),           tags$p(             tags$strong("Blocker state: "),             useBlocker(shouldBlock = should_block, render = blocker_ui)           ),           tags$p(             style = "color: #555; font-size: 0.9em;",             "Click 'Other page'. The state changes to ",             tags$code('"blocked"'), " and two buttons appear.",             tags$br(),             tags$em("Leave"), " calls ", tags$code("b.proceed()"),             " to confirm navigation;",             tags$br(),             tags$em("Stay"), " calls ", tags$code("b.reset()"),             " to cancel and return to ", tags$code('"unblocked"'), "."           )         )       ),       Route(         path = "other",         element = div(           tags$h3("Other page"),           tags$p("No blocker here. Navigate freely.")         )       )     )   ) )

title: Advanced hooks: useBlocker and useOutletContext
text: context passed layout level available every child route without repeating element.
code: library(reactRouter) library(htmltools)  current_user <- list(name = "Alice", role = "admin", email = "alice@example.com")  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       element = div(         tags$nav(tags$ul(           tags$li(NavLink(to = "/",        "Dashboard")),           tags$li(NavLink(to = "/profile", "Profile")),           tags$li(NavLink(to = "/settings","Settings"))         )),         tags$hr(),         Outlet(context = current_user)       ),       Route(         index = TRUE,         element = div(           tags$h3("Dashboard"),           tags$p(             "Welcome back, ",             tags$strong(useOutletContext(tags$span(), selector = "name")),             "!"           ),           tags$p("Your role: ", useOutletContext(tags$code(), selector = "role"))         )       ),       Route(         path = "profile",         element = div(           tags$h3("Profile"),           tags$p("Name: ",  useOutletContext(tags$span(), selector = "name")),           tags$p("Email: ", useOutletContext(tags$span(), selector = "email")),           tags$p("Role: ",  useOutletContext(tags$span(), selector = "role")),           tags$details(             tags$summary("Full context (JSON)"),             tags$pre(useOutletContext(tags$span()))           )         )       ),       Route(         path = "settings",         element = div(           tags$h3("Settings"),           tags$p(             "Editing settings for ",             tags$strong(useOutletContext(tags$span(), selector = "name"))           )         )       )     )   ) )

title: Advanced hooks: useBlocker and useOutletContext
text: selector extracts single field. need compose several fields one output — greeting, formatted label, conditional element — use render instead: matches React Router v7 idiom component decides consume hook value, cost small amount inline JS.
code: useOutletContext(   render = JS(     "u => u.role === 'admin'       ? `${u.name} (admin — ${u.email})`       : u.name"   ) )

title: Introduction to reactRouter
code: # adapted from example of shiny.router # https://github.com/Appsilon/shiny.router/tree/main/examples/shiny_modules library(shiny) library(reactRouter)  # This creates UI for each page. page <- function(title, content, id) {   ns <- NS(id)   div(     titlePanel(title),     p(content),     textOutput(ns("click_me"))   ) }  # Both sample pages. root_page <- page("Home page", "Home page clicks", "root") second_page <- page("Other page", "Other page clicks", "second")  server_module <- function(id, clicks, power = 1) {   moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     output$click_me <- renderText({       as.numeric(clicks())^power     })   }) }  # Create output for our router in main UI of Shiny app. ui <- RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       element = div(         NavLink(to = "/", "Main"), br(),         NavLink(to = "/other", "Other"),         actionButton("clicks", "Click me!"),         Outlet()       ),       Route(index = TRUE, element = div(root_page)),       Route(path = "other", element = div(second_page))     )   ) )  # Plug router into Shiny server. server <- function(input, output, session) {   clicks <- reactive({     input$clicks   })   server_module("root", clicks = clicks, power = 1)   server_module("second", clicks = clicks, power = 2) }  # Run server in a standard way. shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Introduction to reactRouter
text: React Router provides client routing, can easily create multiple routes Quarto R markdown documents:
code: # code to run in a Quarto document # example adapted from: https://github.com/remix-run/react-router/tree/dev/examples/basic library(reactRouter) library(htmltools)  Layout <- div(   style = "border:1px solid black;", # add border just for the example   h1("Basic Example"),   tags$p(     paste0('This example demonstrates some of the core features of React Router         including nested reactRouter::Route(), reactRouter::Outlet(),         reactRouter::Link(), and using a "*" route (aka "splat route")         to render a "not found" page when someone visits an unrecognized URL.'     )   ),   # A "layout route" is a good place to put markup you want to   # share across all the pages on your site, like navigation.   tags$nav(     tags$ul(       tags$li(         reactRouter::Link(to = "/", "Home")       ),       tags$li(         reactRouter::Link(to = "/dashboard", "Dashboard")       ),       tags$li(         reactRouter::Link(to = "/nothing-here", "Nothing Here")       )     )   ),   tags$hr(),   # An <Outlet> renders whatever child route is currently active,   # so you can think about this <Outlet> as a placeholder for   # the child routes we defined above.   reactRouter::Outlet() )  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       element = Layout,       Route(         index = TRUE,         element = div(           tags$h2("Home"),           tags$p("Home content")         )       ),       Route(         path = "dashboard",         element = div(           tags$h2("Dashboard"),           tags$p("Dashboard here")         )       ),       # Using path="*"" means "match anything", so this route       # acts like a catch-all for URLs that we don't have explicit       # routes for.       Route(         path = "*",         element = div(           tags$h2("Nothing to see here!"),           tags$p(             # reactRouter::Link() conflicts with muiMaterial::Link()             reactRouter::Link(to = "/", "Go to the home page")           )         )       )     )   ) )

title: Choosing a router
text: three create*Router() functions differ interact browser URL:

title: Choosing a router
text: createHashRouter() uses URL hash (/#/) routing. Hash changes never trigger real HTTP request, router works Shiny apps, Quarto documents, static HTML pages without server configuration. works well Shiny: Hash changes affect Shiny’s HTTP layer. session$clientData$url_hash updates reactively whenever route changes. preferred legacy HashRouter(): Unlocks full data router API: loader, action, errorElement, useLoaderData, useNavigation, useFetcher, deferred data via Await. model React Router actively developing; HashRouter() legacy compatibility wrapper.
code: library(reactRouter) library(htmltools)  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       element = div(         tags$nav(           tags$ul(             tags$li(NavLink(to = "/",      "Home")),             tags$li(NavLink(to = "/about", "About"))           )         ),         tags$hr(),         Outlet()       ),       Route(index = TRUE,   element = div(h2("Home"),  p("Welcome."))),       Route(path = "about", element = div(h2("About"), p("About page.")))     )   ) )

title: Choosing a router
text: createMemoryRouter() keeps routing state entirely memory. never reads modifies browser URL, means: always starts /, regardless real URL. Navigation updates -memory location browser address bar change. Browser back/forward buttons bookmarks reflect current route. makes ideal internal-navigation — multi-step wizards, tabbed panels, UI route implementation detail rather something user share bookmark. also right choice need multiple independent routers page (e.g. several routed widgets Shiny dashboard), since don’t touch URL therefore can’t conflict . Choosing hash memory routers: short: route visible URL (bookmarks, deep links, back/forward), use createHashRouter(). routing purely internal UI concern, use createMemoryRouter().
code: library(reactRouter) library(htmltools)  RouterProvider(   router = createMemoryRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       element = div(         tags$nav(           tags$ul(             tags$li(NavLink(to = "/",      "Home")),             tags$li(NavLink(to = "/about", "About"))           )         ),         tags$hr(),         Outlet()       ),       Route(index = TRUE,   element = div(h2("Home"),  p("Welcome."))),       Route(path = "about", element = div(h2("About"), p("About page.")))     )   ) )

title: Choosing a router
text: createBrowserRouter() uses HTML5 History API (pushState) clean URLs (e.g. /instead /#/). standard router full React web applications, well-suited use R package: Static files (file://): URL path file system path (e.g. /C:/Users/.../file.html), /. route matches app immediately shows 404. Shiny apps: Clean URLs like /require server rewrite paths back /. Shiny default. Refreshing sub-route: Even served proper HTTP server, refreshing /404 unless server configured rewrite routes index.html. createBrowserRouter() works correctly full web application deployment (e.g. React served Node.js, Express, Nginx URL rewriting). scenario outside scope R package. nevertheless decide use createBrowserRouter() Shiny app mounted sub-path (e.g. Posit Connect /content/abc/ behind reverse proxy), set basename router knows app starts. Otherwise every route 404s router compares full path (/content/abc/) patterns rooted /. createHashRouter() createMemoryRouter() need basename — neither uses URL path.
code: RouterProvider(   router = createBrowserRouter(     Route(path = "/", element = ...),     basename = "/content/abc"   # match your deployment prefix   ) )

what: Legacy: HashRouter() and MemoryRouter() — component API
title: Choosing a router
text: HashRouter() MemoryRouter() older, component-based equivalents createHashRouter() createMemoryRouter(). remain fully supported safe use existing code. However, RouterProvider() createHashRouter() / createMemoryRouter() preferred new code component-based API: support loader, action, errorElement, data router features. direction React Router developing toward. Use HashRouter() MemoryRouter() specific reason stay component API (e.g. migrating existing app incrementally).

previous_headings: Legacy: HashRouter() and MemoryRouter() — component API
what: Legacy router caveat: re-rendering parent UI remounts route elements
title: Choosing a router
text: Route() attaches fresh random React key element every R-side call. data router (createHashRouter() / RouterProvider()) builds route tree mount unaffected. component-API routers (HashRouter() / MemoryRouter() + Routes() + Route()) different: host UI re-rendered — typically via shiny::renderUI() / shiny::uiOutput() — Route() re-evaluated, every route element gets new key, React unmounts remounts matched element every parent render even URL changed. client-side state inside route element (form input, scroll position, third-party widget state) reset. Workarounds: Preferred: switch RouterProvider(router = createHashRouter(...)). data router builds route tree immune. must keep legacy component API, render router top level ui (inside renderUI) built .

title: Choosing a router
text: Link() NavLink() accept reloadDocument prop. default FALSE, matching React Router’s default, default correct almost every use package — including Shiny apps server-rendered output.

title: Choosing a router
text: React Router intercepts click updates route entirely client side, without reloading page. right behavior : Static sites Quarto documents — server re-initialize. createMemoryRouter() / MemoryRouter() — full page reload reset -memory routing state back /. Data router loader / action — run client-side navigations; full reload bypasses . Shiny apps uiOutput, renderUI, plotOutput, htmlwidgets. Shiny output bindings re-attach automatically React Router mounts new route’s element, reactives depend URL — useLocation(), useParams(), useSearchParams(), Shiny’s reactive session$clientData$url_hash — update without reload. bundled shiny.fluent example (reactRouterExample("shiny.fluent")) navigates routes containing live echarts4rOutput() uiOutput() uses default reloadDocument = FALSE throughout. full reload actually worse Shiny: tears session, drops shared reactives (e.g. hero_selected reactive used across routes), re-runs every initialization step every click.

title: Choosing a router
text: Setting reloadDocument = TRUE skips React Router’s client-side navigation lets browser handle <> click natively. actually depends router: practice means reloadDocument = TRUE rarely right choice package. Leave default.

title: Security considerations
text: reactRouter wrapper around React Router, routing logic runs user’s browser, Shiny server. Routes, loader = JS(...), action = JS(...), JS() snippet part page ships every visitor. can read , edit browser’s developer tools, call URL calls. Almost every recommendation follows single fact.

title: Security considerations
text: Anything put inside JS() string visible anyone opens page. includes API keys, tokens, database credentials. loader needs authenticated data, fetch Shiny server (API control) let server attach credential.
code: library(reactRouter)  # NOT OK — the API key is shipped to every visitor Route(   path = "/private",   loader = JS("async () => {     const r = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data', {       headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer sk-LIVE-1234...' }     });     return r.json();   }"),   element = useLoaderData(tags$pre()) )

what: 2. Authentication belongs to Shiny, not the router
title: Security considerations
text: loader like (!isLoggedIn()) return redirect("/login") convenient, isn’t real lock — curious user can just delete check browser reach route. real protection, check user can’t tamper : front Shiny — Posit Connect, Shinyapps.io, ShinyProxy, reverse proxy requires login app loads. Inside Shiny server — packages like shinymanager, checks session$user. reactRouter good fit showing login screen “’re allowed see ” page, decision whether user allowed see data made server. useful test: removed every JS() check app, user see anything shouldn’t? yes, check wrong place.

what: 3. React escapes text — keep it that way
title: Security considerations
text: display loader action data useLoaderData() / useActionData(), React renders plain text. Even value contains HTML <script> tags, shows literal characters, code. get protection free. way lose protection opt explicitly dangerouslySetInnerHTML custom JavaScript. Don’t, unless ’ve sanitised input first (e.g. DOMPurify).
code: # Safe: this renders as the literal text "" Route( path = "/profile", loader = JS("async () => ({ bio: '' })"), element = useLoaderData(tags$div(), selector = "bio") )

what: 4. Encode URL parameters in fetch() calls
title: Security considerations
text: loader takes path parameter (like :id) pastes URL, wrap encodeURIComponent(). Otherwise crafted link can append extra query parameters path segments upstream request. applies values read useSearchParams() go URL.
code: # Vulnerable loader_bad <- JS("async ({ params }) => {   const r = await fetch(`https://api.example.com/films/${params.id}`);   return r.json(); }")  # Safe loader_good <- JS("async ({ params }) => {   const id = encodeURIComponent(params.id);   const r  = await fetch(`https://api.example.com/films/${id}`);   return r.json(); }")

title: Security considerations
text: redirect() blocks dangerous URL schemes javascript: data:, protocol-relative targets like //evil.example.com/path (browser otherwise interpret absolute URL another origin): genuinely need cross-origin redirect, spell full https://... URL. ever comes user input, still don’t rely built-checks alone – match allowlist. build redirect target user input (example ?next= parameter sign-), ’s safer compare list allowed paths try filter string :
code: redirect("javascript:alert(1)") #> Error: redirect(): refusing unsafe URL scheme in `to` = "javascript:alert(1)". redirect("//evil.example.com/path") #> Error: redirect(): refusing protocol-relative URL in `to` = "//evil.example.com/path". ... nextParam  <- "/dashboard"   # in real code, from useSearchParams() allowedNext <- c("/", "/dashboard", "/profile", "/reports")  target <- if (nextParam %in% allowedNext) nextParam else "/" Route(path = "/post-login", loader = redirect(target), element = NULL)

what: 6. A note on Form and action
title: Security considerations
text: reactRouter exposes Form component route actions completeness, also run browser. use save data, actual write needs happen server endpoint control — ’s authentication, validation, storage belong. Shiny apps, sticking regular Shiny inputs observeEvent() server simpler safer.

title: Security considerations
text: ’re hosting app want extra layer defence, add Content-Security-Policy header. Among things, lets list exactly external APIs app allowed talk , bug elsewhere code can’t quietly send data third party. starting point Shiny app: 'unsafe-inline' scripts currently needed Shiny (client uses inline scripts bootstrap session); reactRouter require . future Shiny release tightens , policy can drop 'unsafe-inline'.
code: Content-Security-Policy:   default-src 'self';   script-src  'self' 'unsafe-inline';   style-src   'self' 'unsafe-inline';   connect-src 'self' https://api.example.com;   img-src     'self' data: https:;   frame-ancestors 'none';

title: Tutorial: Using reactRouter with rhino and shiny.fluent
text: Note. tutorial uses legacy component-based router (HashRouter() + Routes()) compatibility rhino’s box module loading. new projects, prefer data router API (RouterProvider() + createHashRouter()) shown vignette("routers", package = "reactRouter"), unlocks loader, action, errorElement, full hook surface. tutorial demonstrates build dynamic Shiny application using reactRouter routing shiny.fluent modern UI components, within rhino framework. ’ll use Dota 2 API data example routing multiple pages. Initially, ensure necessary rhino package installed. can running following command R console: Next, need create new rhino project. haven’t already set rhino project, can running following command R console: create basic structure application. Add following libraries dependencies.R file: Now ready go.
code: # Install rhino if not yet installed install.packages("rhino") # Initialize a new rhino project (will create project scaffolding) rhino::init() # dependencies.R  library(rhino) # App structure library(httr) # API requests library(shiny.fluent) # UI components library(reactRouter) # Client-side routing library(echarts4r) # Charting library(stringdist) # String matching library(treesitter) # Optional: Syntax parsing library(treesitter.r) # Optional: R syntax support renv::snapshot()

title: Tutorial: Using reactRouter with rhino and shiny.fluent
text: example, create simple application displays information Dota 2 heroes. application multiple routes, allowing users navigate different pages. components application structured follows: home: main page application, display list heroes menu: navigation menu application, allowing users navigate different pages header: header component displayed every page details: page containing detailed information specific hero benchmark: page display benchmark statistics heroes ranks: page display ranks heroes based performance final strucutre app look like : main part application address routing UI components app.R file. function defines overall layout routing application using reactRouter. contains four key parts: Top-level routing via HashRouter() Routes(). Root Route /: Displays home page. Nested Route /:projectId/*: Displays layout (menu) child routes (details, benchmark, rank). sub-route renders different module UI (e.g., detailsui,benchmarkui, benchmarkui). Fallback * Route: Catches undefined paths shows custom 404 message.
code: ├── app │   ├── js │   │   └── index.js │   ├── logic │   │   ├── data.R │   │   └── utils.R │   ├── main.R │   ├── static │   │   ├── css │   │   │   └── app.min.css │   │   ├── favicon.ico │   │   └── js │   │       └── app.min.js │   ├── styles │   │   └── main.scss │   └── view │       ├── benchmark.R │       ├── details.R │       ├── header.R │       ├── home.R │       ├── menu.R │       └── rank.R ├── app.R ├── config.yml ├── dependencies.R ├── renv.lock ├── rhino.yml └── run_dev.R # app / main.R  box::use(   app / view / home,   app / view / menu,   app / view / details,   app / view / benchmark,   app / view / rank )   # Define UI with namespaced modules ui <- function(id) {   ns <- shiny::NS(id) # Namespace for module isolation   shiny.fluent::fluentPage(     reactRouter::HashRouter(       reactRouter::Routes(         # Home page route         reactRouter::Route(path = "/", element = home$ui(ns("home"))),          # Project-based nested routes         reactRouter::Route(           path = "/:projectId/*",           element = menu$ui(ns("menu")), # Common layout/menu           children = list(             reactRouter::Route(               path = "details",               element = details$ui(ns("details"))             ),             reactRouter::Route(               path = "benchmark",               element = benchmark$ui(ns("benchmark"))             ),             reactRouter::Route(               path = "rank",               element = rank$ui(ns("rank"))             )           )         ),          # Fallback for undefined routes         reactRouter::Route(path = "*", element = "Custom error 404")       )     )   ) }  #' @export server <- function(id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     hero_selected <- home$server("home")      shiny::observe({       shiny::req(hero_selected())        print(paste0("hero_id selected: ", hero_selected()))     })      menu$server("menu", hero_selected = hero_selected)     details$server("details", hero_selected = hero_selected)     benchmark$server("benchmark", hero_selected = hero_selected)     rank$server("rank", hero_selected = hero_selected)   }) }

what: Form() with GET — search and useLoaderData
title: Using Form, useFetcher, useFetchers, and useRevalidator
text: GET Form() updates URL’s search params triggers route’s loader. Combine useLoaderData() display results useSearchParams() reflect current query.
code: library(reactRouter) library(htmltools)  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       element = div(         tags$h2("Search Example"),         Form(           method = "get",           action = "/search",           tags$input(type = "search", name = "q", placeholder = "Search..."),           tags$button(type = "submit", "Search")         ),         Outlet()       ),       Route(index = TRUE, element = div(tags$p("Enter a query above."))),       Route(         path = "search",         loader = JS("({ request }) => {           const q = new URL(request.url).searchParams.get('q') || '';           const db = ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Charlie', 'David', 'Eve'];           const hits = db.filter(n => n.toLowerCase().includes(q.toLowerCase()));           return { count: hits.length, results: hits.join(', '), query: q };         }"),         element = div(           tags$p("Query: ",   useSearchParams(tags$strong(), param = "q")),           tags$p("Results: ", useLoaderData(tags$strong(),   selector = "count")),           tags$pre(           useLoaderData(tags$span(),     selector = "results"))         )       )     )   ) )

text: useFetcher() reads state fetcher instance. useful need observe loading submission state independently — e.g. show spinner next inline element without affecting global navigation state. Use Form(navigate = FALSE, fetcherKey = "...") submit data without page-level navigation. use useFetcher() key read fetcher’s state data.

title: Using Form, useFetcher, useFetchers, and useRevalidator

The URL does not change.",           style = "color: #555;"         ),         Form(           navigate  = FALSE,           fetcherKey = "search",           method    = "get",           action    = "/search",           style     = "display: flex; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 16px;",           tags$input(             type = "search", name = "q", placeholder = "Search names...",             style = "padding: 6px; font-size: 14px;"           ),           tags$button(type = "submit", "Search", style = "padding: 6px 12px; cursor: pointer;")         ),         tags$p(           tags$strong("Fetcher state: "),           useFetcher(tags$span(), fetcherKey = "search", selector = "state")         ),         tags$p(           tags$strong("Matches found: "),           useFetcher(tags$span(), fetcherKey = "search", selector = "data.count")         ),         tags$details(           tags$summary("Raw results (JSON)"),           tags$pre(useFetcher(tags$span(), fetcherKey = "search", selector = "data.results"))         ),         tags$hr(),         Outlet()       ),       Route(index = TRUE, element = div(tags$p("Enter a name above and click Search."))),       Route(         path = "search",         loader = JS(sprintf(           "async ({ request }) => {             await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 800));             const q = new URL(request.url).searchParams.get('q') || '';             const db = %s;             const hits = db.filter(r => r.name.toLowerCase().includes(q.toLowerCase()));             return { count: hits.length, results: hits };           }",           people_json         )),         element = div()       )     )   ) )

title: Using Form, useFetcher, useFetchers, and useRevalidator
text: useFetchers() returns currently active fetchers. useful global loading indicator shows whenever background fetch progress, regardless component started .

what: Full example: monitor all active fetchers
title: Using Form, useFetcher, useFetchers, and useRevalidator
text: fetchers active, useFetchers(selector = "state") renders "[]".
code: library(reactRouter) library(htmltools) library(jsonlite)  people_json <- jsonlite::toJSON(   data.frame(name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie", "David", "Eve")),   dataframe = "rows",   auto_unbox = TRUE )  loader_js <- JS(sprintf(   "async ({ request }) => {     await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1000));     const q = new URL(request.url).searchParams.get('q') || '';     const db = %s;     return db.filter(r => r.name.toLowerCase().includes(q.toLowerCase()));   }",   people_json ))  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       element = div(         tags$h2("useFetchers Example — Global Loading Indicator"),         tags$p(           "Submit one or more search forms. Each uses a different fetcher key.",           style = "color: #555;"         ),         tags$p(           tags$strong("All fetcher states: "),           useFetchers(tags$code(), selector = "state")         ),         tags$hr(),          tags$h3("Search A"),         Form(           navigate = FALSE, fetcherKey = "search-a",           method = "get", action = "/search",           style = "display: flex; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 8px;",           tags$input(type = "search", name = "q", placeholder = "Name...", style = "padding: 4px;"),           tags$button(type = "submit", "Search A", style = "cursor: pointer;")         ),         tags$p("State A: ",   useFetcher(tags$code(), fetcherKey = "search-a", selector = "state")),         tags$p("Results A: ", useFetcher(tags$span(), fetcherKey = "search-a", selector = "data")),          tags$h3("Search B"),         Form(           navigate = FALSE, fetcherKey = "search-b",           method = "get", action = "/search",           style = "display: flex; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 8px;",           tags$input(type = "search", name = "q", placeholder = "Name...", style = "padding: 4px;"),           tags$button(type = "submit", "Search B", style = "cursor: pointer;")         ),         tags$p("State B: ",   useFetcher(tags$code(), fetcherKey = "search-b", selector = "state")),         tags$p("Results B: ", useFetcher(tags$span(), fetcherKey = "search-b", selector = "data")),          tags$hr(),         Outlet()       ),       Route(index = TRUE, element = div()),       Route(path = "search", loader = loader_js, element = div())     )   ) )

title: Using useLoaderData
text: useLoaderData() connects React Router’s useLoaderData() hook UI. calls hook injects result component — either text content inside plain HTML element, structured JavaScript value prop shiny.react-based component. Use inside Route loader function.

title: Using useLoaderData
text: Pass htmltools tag render loader data text. simplest use case — extra configuration needed: Full example dynamic route parameters:
code: # Render a single field as text useLoaderData(tags$h3(), selector = "name")      # renders the "name" field useLoaderData(tags$pre())                         # renders full JSON string library(reactRouter) library(htmltools) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  people_json <- jsonlite::toJSON(dplyr::starwars, dataframe = "rows", auto_unbox = TRUE)  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       element = tags$div(         NavLink(to = "/", "Home"), " | ",         NavLink(to = "/people/1", "Luke"), " | ",         NavLink(to = "/people/2", "C-3PO"), " | ",         NavLink(to = "/people/3", "R2-D2"),         tags$hr(),         Outlet()       ),       Route(index = TRUE, element = p("Select a person.")),       Route(         path = "people/:id",         loader = JS(sprintf(           "({ params }) => {             const db = %s;             const person = db[params.id - 1];             if (!person) throw new Response('Not found', { status: 404 });             return person;           }",           people_json         )),         element = div(           useLoaderData(into = tags$h3(), selector = "name"),           useLoaderData(into = tags$p(),  selector = "gender")         ),         errorElement = div(tags$p("Not found."), NavLink(to = "/", "Back"))       )     )   ) )

what: Injecting structured data into shiny.react components
title: Using useLoaderData
text: Pass shiny.react-based component specify target prop . loader data (selector field ) injected raw JavaScript value — converted string. needed whenever component expects actual array object prop, example rows data grid options autocomplete. loader returns object multiple fields, use selector extract relevant one:
code: library(reactRouter) library(muiDataGrid) library(htmltools)  loader <- JS("async () => {   const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');   return (await res.json()).map((p, i) => ({     id: i, name: p.name, height: p.height   })); }")  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       loader = loader,       element = div(         style = "height: 400px;",         useLoaderData(           muiDataGrid::DataGrid(             columns = list(               list(field = "name", headerName = "Name", flex = 1),               list(field = "height", headerName = "Height", width = 100)             )           ),           as = "rows"         )       )     )   ) ) # -- JS loader: fetches Star Wars characters from SWAPI, filters by ?name= param ---- loader_people <- JS(   "   async ({ request }) => {     const filter = new URL(request.url).searchParams.getAll('name');     const data = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people').then(r => r.json());     return {       names: data.map(p => p.name).sort(),       people: filter.length ? data.filter(p => filter.includes(p.name)) : data     };   } " )  # -- UI -------------------------------------------------------------------- RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       loader = loader_people,       element = div(         h2("Star Wars Characters"),         p(           "Select characters to filter the table, or clear the selection to show all."         ),         useLoaderData(           into = muiMaterial::Autocomplete(             onChange = JS(               "(event, value) => { window.location.hash = value.length ? '/?' + value.map(v => 'name=' + encodeURIComponent(v)).join('&') : '/'; }"             ),             renderInput = JS(               "(params) => React.createElement(window.jsmodule['@mui/material'].TextField, {...params, label: 'Name'})"             ),             multiple = TRUE,             sx = list(width = 300, marginBottom = 2)           ),           as = "options",           selector = "names"         ),         useLoaderData(           into = muiDataGrid::DataGrid(             columns = list(               list(field = 'name', headerName = 'Name', flex = 1),               list(field = 'height', headerName = 'Height', width = 100),               list(field = 'mass', headerName = 'Mass', width = 100),               list(field = 'gender', headerName = 'Gender', width = 120),               list(field = 'birth_year', headerName = 'Birth Year', width = 120)             ),             getRowId = JS("function(row) { return(row.name) }"),             initialState = list(               pagination = list(paginationModel = list(pageSize = 5))             ),             showToolbar = TRUE           ),           as = "rows",           selector = "people"         )       )     )   ) )

title: Using useLoaderData
text: React Router community recommends loader returns exactly shape consuming component needs. avoids need selector: single route renders multiple components different data needs, consider splitting nested routes — loader:
code: library(reactRouter) library(muiDataGrid) library(htmltools)  stats_loader <- JS("async () => {   const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');   const people = await res.json();   return { summary: people.length + ' characters found' }; }")  people_loader <- JS("async () => {   const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');   return (await res.json()).map((p, i) => ({     id: i, name: p.name, height: p.height   })); }")  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       loader = stats_loader,       element = div(         useLoaderData(tags$h2(), selector = "summary"),         Outlet()       ),       Route(         index = TRUE,         loader = people_loader,         element = div(           style = "height: 400px;",           useLoaderData(             into = muiDataGrid::DataGrid(               columns = list(                 list(field = "name", "headerName" = "Name", flex = 1),                 list(field = "height", "headerName" = "Height", width = 100)               )             ),            as = "rows")         )       )     )   ) )

title: Using useLoaderData
text: useLoaderData() works component built shiny.react — simply injects prop value via React.cloneElement(). Examples compatible packages: muiMaterial — MUI Material UI components muiDataGrid — MUI Data Grid muiCharts — MUI Charts muiTreeView — MUI Tree View shiny.fluent — Microsoft Fluent UI components shiny.blueprint — Palantir Blueprint UI components Standard htmltools tags (tags$div, tags$h3, etc.) support text injection (default = "children" structured data). muiCharts::BarChart — inject loader data dataset:
code: library(reactRouter) library(muiCharts) library(htmltools)  loader <- JS("async () => {   const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');   const people = await res.json();   return people.slice(0, 10).map(p => ({     name: p.name,     height: Number(p.height) || 0,     mass: Number(p.mass) || 0   })); }")  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       loader = loader,       element = useLoaderData(         muiCharts::BarChart(           series = list(             list(dataKey = "height", label = "Height (cm)"),             list(dataKey = "mass",   label = "Mass (kg)")           ),           xAxis = list(list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "name")),           height = 400         ),         as = "dataset"       )     )   ) )

title: Using useLoaderData
text: inject loader data one prop component, nest useLoaderData() calls: call extracts selector useLoaderData() result injects specified prop.
code: library(reactRouter) library(muiDataGrid) library(htmltools)  loader <- JS("async () => {   const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');   const people = await res.json();   return {     columnDefs: [       { field: 'name',   headerName: 'Name',   flex: 1 },       { field: 'height', headerName: 'Height', width: 100 }     ],     people: people.map((p, i) => ({ id: i, name: p.name, height: p.height }))   }; }")  RouterProvider(   router = createHashRouter(     Route(       path = "/",       loader = loader,       element = div(         style = "height: 400px;",         useLoaderData(           useLoaderData(             muiDataGrid::DataGrid(),             as = "rows",             selector = "people"           ),           as = "columns",           selector = "columnDefs"         )       )     )   ) )

title: Await — Await
text: component (HTML tag shiny.react-based element) receive hook data specified prop. Character. name component's prop inject hook data (default "children" text display, "rows" data grid, "value" input). resolveKey Character. key loader's return value holds promise (e.g. loader returns { data: promise }, set resolveKey = "data"). selector Character. Optional key extract hook data object. NULL (default), entire data passed. Dotted paths like "summary.title" navigate nested objects. render Optional JS function (value) => ReactNode used place /. Mirrors native React Router pattern cases single prop expressive enough (e.g. JS("v => `${v.first} ${v.last}`")). errorElement Element render promise rejects. fallback Element shown promise pending. Defaults plain "Loading\u2026" span. ... Additional props pass component.

title: Await — Await
text: Renders deferred loader promise resolves, injecting resolved value (selector ) prop. Use inside Route whose loader returns object containing promise (written via JS). React Router v7, simply return object directly – defer() wrapper needed.

title: reactRouter 0.2.0
text: CRAN submission. Major release upgrading package React Router v7 data router API (loaders, actions, fetchers, deferred data).

title: reactRouter 0.2.0
text: RouterProvider() now takes router argument built create*Router() instead route children. data() renamed dataResponse() replace() renamed replaceResponse() avoid masking base R functions. reloadDocument Link() / NavLink() defaults FALSE.

title: reactRouter 0.2.0
text: Updated React Router v7 (react-router-dom 7.x). Data router API: createBrowserRouter(), createHashRouter(), createMemoryRouter() paired RouterProvider(router = ...) — recommended way use loaders, actions, fetchers, Await. New components: Await, Form, ScrollRestoration, Outlet, Routes, Navigate. New hooks: useLoaderData, useActionData, useNavigation, useNavigate, useNavigationType, useMatch, useMatches, useSearchParams, useRouteError, useRouteLoaderData, useFetcher, useFetchers, useRevalidator, useBlocker, useSubmit, . New loader/action helpers: redirect(), replaceResponse(), redirectDocument(), dataResponse().

title: reactRouter 0.2.0
text: redirect(), replaceResponse(), redirectDocument() reject unsafe URL schemes (javascript:, data:, vbscript:) protocol-relative targets. reactRouterExample() validates example list bundled examples, closing path-traversal vector user-supplied input. New vignette: “Security considerations” — guidance loaders/actions client-side code, URL-encoding route params, redirect targets, CSP, .

title: reactRouter 0.2.0
text: Route() validates loader action inherit JS_EVAL call time, surfacing common mistake becomes browser-side error. dataResponse(): value now required argument. useNavigate(), useSubmit(), useLinkClickHandler() refuse = = "children" point render = JS(...) = "onClick" — hooks return functions, renderable children. RouterProvider logs dev-mode warning route tree changes mount; remount via key prop apply new routes.
